content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commissioner
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-9144

August 9, 1983

Russell L. Schweickart
392 La Sierra Drive
Sacramento, CA 95825

RE: Your Request for Advice, Our
Advise No. A-83-135

Dear Mr. Schweickart:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on the
conflict of interests provisions of the Political Reform Act. I
understand the facts, as stated in your letter, to be as
follows: You are a Commissioner for the California Energy
Commission. You have been asked to serve on the board of
directors of a political action committee that is being
organized to aid Senatorial candidates who support international
arms control and limitations on nuclear weapons proliferation.
You do not anticipate receiving any income from this activity,
but the committee may reimburse you for any expenses that you
incur in serving on the board. Your question is whether your
acceptance of a position on the board of directors will create
any conflict of interests with your current position.

Based upon a review of the applicable provisions of the
Government Code, and based upon the analysis of our staff
attorneys, we conclude that there is no prohibition within our
jurisdiction against your accepting the position. Additionally,
you will not be placed in a conflict of interest position unless
you receive $250 or more in reimbursements from the political
action committee and you are asked, as a member of the
California Energy Commission, to make a decision which will
foreseeably have some material financial effect on the political
action committee. If you receive $250 or more in reimbursements
from the political action committee, you will be required to
report the committee on your Statement of Economic Interests.

Therefore, there are no proscriptions against your serving
as a member of the board of directors of the proposed political
action committee and no conflicts of interest are created unless

Mr. Russell L. Schweickart
August 9, 1983
Page 2


you receive $250 or more in reimbursements from the committee
and you are asked to make a decision which will financially
affect the political action committee. Under these latter
circumstances, you would, of course, be required to disqualify
yourself from any such decision.

I hope this information will be of some assistance to you.
If you have any questions or comments, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,


Dan Stanford
Chairman

DLS:nrw

RUSSELL L SCHWEICKART
892 LA SIERRA DRIVE
SACRAMENTO, CA 95825


2 August 1983

Mr. Donald Stanford
Chairman, FPPC
1100 K St., P.O.Box 507
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Stanford:

     I have been invited to serve on the board of directors of
a political action committee currently in the formative stages.
It is being organized to aid Senatorial candidates who support
international arms control and limitations on nuclear weapon
proliferation. It is my understanding that I will receive no
income from this activity but that expenses incurred in serving
on the board may be reimbursed. I see no conflict whatsoever
with my responsibilities for the state in my capacity as
Commissioner at the California Energy Commission.

     I would appreciate your advice on accepting this position
via via FPPC rules and the state's conflict of interest laws.

Thank you for attention to this matter,

Sincerely,


Russell L. Schweickart

sections.question: Your question is whether your acceptance of a position on the board of directors will create any conflict of interests with your current position.
sections.conclusion: Based upon a review of the applicable provisions of the Government Code, and based upon the analysis of our staff attorneys, we conclude that there is no prohibition within our jurisdiction against your accepting the position. Additionally, you will not be placed in a conflict of interest position unless you receive $250 or more in reimbursements from the political action committee and you are asked, as a member of the California Energy Commission, to make a decision which will foreseeably have some material financial effect on the political action committee. If you receive $250 or more in reimbursements from the political action committee, you will be required to file a Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether accepting a position on the board of directors of a political action committee supporting senatorial candidates who favor international arms control would create a conflict of interest with the requestor's position as a California Energy Commission Commissioner.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes there is no prohibition against accepting the position. A conflict of interest arises only if the requestor receives $250 or more in reimbursements and must make a decision at the Energy Commission with material financial effect on the political action committee, in which case disqualification would be required.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request and FPPC response. OCR quality issues present but core content extracted. Question and conclusion sections clearly identifiable. Referenced as Advice No. 83-135.

QUESTION: Your question is whether your acceptance of a position on the board of directors will create any conflict of interests with your current position.

CONCLUSION: Based upon a review of the applicable provisions of the Government Code, and based upon the analysis of our staff attorneys, we conclude that there is no prohibition within our jurisdiction against your accepting the position. Additionally, you will not be placed in a conflict of interest position unless you receive $250 or more in reimbursements from the political action committee and you are asked, as a member of the California Energy Commission, to make a decision which will foreseeably have some material financial effect on the political action committee. If you receive $250 or more in reimbursements from the political action committee, you will be required to file a Statement of Economic Interests.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter 83-135 addresses whether a California Energy Commission Commissioner may serve on the board of directors of a political action committee supporting arms control-focused senatorial candidates without creating a conflict of interest. The FPPC concludes no conflict exists unless reimbursements exceed $250 and the Commissioner must make a financially affecting decision.
